title: Normalize email case in user registration
description:
Repository: https://github.com/MuhammedSenn/demo-user-service-python
Branch: main

Requirement:
register_user in registration.py treats emails as case-sensitive, so the same address with different letter casing (for example User@Example.com and user@example.com) can register twice. Normalize the email by trimming surrounding whitespace and lowercasing it before the duplicate check and before storing it, so duplicates are detected case-insensitively and the stored value is the normalized form. Keep the existing empty-email and invalid-format validation behavior unchanged.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Registering User@Example.com and then user@example.com raises ValueError with message 'User already registered'
- A successfully registered email is stored in normalized (trimmed, lowercased) form
- Existing empty-email and invalid-format validation still works
- Add or update unit tests in test_registration.py covering case-insensitive duplicate detection
